q: Let a differentiable function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi></math> satisfy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>t</mi></mfenced></mrow><mi>t</mi></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>3</mn></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>8</mn></mfenced></math> is equal to:
question: Let a differentiable function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi></math> satisfy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>t</mi></mfenced></mrow><mi>t</mi></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>3</mn></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>8</mn></mfenced></math> is equal to:
solution:
Given:

fx+∫3xfttdt=x+1

⇒f'x+fxx=12x+1

Put y=fx, then

dydx+yx=12x+1

So, I.F.=e∫dxx=elnx=x

Hence, solution is

xy=12∫xx+1dx

⇒xy=12∫x+1-1x+1dx

⇒xy=12∫x+1-1x+1dx

⇒xy=1223x+132-2x+1+C

⇒xy=13x+132-x+1+C

Put x=3, then f3=2

So,

6=83-2+C⇒C=163

Hence,

xy=13x+132-x+1+163

So, put x=8, then

8f8=273-3+163

⇒f8=343×8

⇒12f8=17

q: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>12</mn></mfrac><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></munderover><mfrac><mrow><mn>8</mn><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cot</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>3</mn></msup></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> equals
question: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>12</mn></mfrac><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></munderover><mfrac><mrow><mn>8</mn><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cot</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>3</mn></msup></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> equals
solution:
Given

∫π12π48cos2xtanx+cotx3dx=∫π12π48cos2xsinxcosx+cosxsinx3dx

=∫π12π4cos2x1sin2x3=∫π12π4cos2x·sin2x·sin22xdx

=14∫π12π4sin4x·(1−cos4x)dx

=14∫π12π4sin4x-18∫π12π4sin8x

=−116cos4xπ12π4+18×8cos8xπ12π4

=−116−1−12+1641+12

=15128.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\displaystyle\int_{-2}^{2} (|\sin x| + [x \sin x])\,dx = 2(3 - \cos 2) + \beta$, where $[\cdot]$ is the greatest integer function. Then $\beta \sin\left(\dfrac{\beta}{2}\right)$ equals:
question: Let $\displaystyle\int_{-2}^{2} (|\sin x| + [x \sin x])\,dx = 2(3 - \cos 2) + \beta$, where $[\cdot]$ is the greatest integer function. Then $\beta \sin\left(\dfrac{\beta}{2}\right)$ equals:
solution:
Let $I = \displaystyle\int_{-2}^{2} \left(|\sin x| + [x \sin x]\right) dx = I_1 + I_2$, where
$I_1 = \displaystyle\int_{-2}^{2} |\sin x|\, dx$ and $I_2 = \displaystyle\int_{-2}^{2} [x \sin x]\, dx$.

Evaluating $I_1$:
Since $|\sin x|$ is even, $I_1 = 2\displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} |\sin x|\, dx$.
For $x \in [0, 2]$, $2 < \pi$, so $\sin x \geq 0$ and $|\sin x| = \sin x$.
$I_1 = 2\displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} \sin x\, dx = 2\left[-\cos x\right]_0^2 = 2(1 - \cos 2) = 2 - 2\cos 2$

Evaluating $I_2$:
Let $g(x) = x\sin x$. Then $g(-x) = (-x)\sin(-x) = x\sin x = g(x)$, so $g(x)$ is even, and hence $[x\sin x]$ is also even.
$I_2 = 2\displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} [x\sin x]\, dx$

Behaviour of $y = x\sin x$ on $[0, 2]$:
At $x = 0$, $y = 0$. At $x = 2$, $y = 2\sin 2 \approx 1.818$.
$y' = \sin x + x\cos x > 0$ on $[0, 2]$, so $y$ is strictly increasing on this interval.

Therefore, $x\sin x$ takes each value in $[0, 1.818]$ exactly once, and crosses $1$ at some unique $\alpha \in (0, 2)$ with $\alpha\sin\alpha = 1$.

For $x \in [0, \alpha)$: $0 \leq x\sin x < 1 \Rightarrow [x\sin x] = 0$
For $x \in [\alpha, 2]$: $1 \leq x\sin x < 2 \Rightarrow [x\sin x] = 1$

$I_2 = 2\left(\displaystyle\int_{0}^{\alpha} 0\, dx + \displaystyle\int_{\alpha}^{2} 1\, dx\right) = 2(2 - \alpha) = 4 - 2\alpha$

Combining:
$I = I_1 + I_2 = (2 - 2\cos 2) + (4 - 2\alpha) = 6 - 2\cos 2 - 2\alpha$

Given $I = 2(3 - \cos 2) + \beta = 6 - 2\cos 2 + \beta$, so:
$6 - 2\cos 2 - 2\alpha = 6 - 2\cos 2 + \beta \Rightarrow \beta = -2\alpha$

Now:
$\beta \sin\left(\dfrac{\beta}{2}\right) = -2\alpha \sin\left(\dfrac{-2\alpha}{2}\right) = -2\alpha \sin(-\alpha) = 2\alpha\sin\alpha$

Since $\alpha\sin\alpha = 1$:
$\beta \sin\left(\dfrac{\beta}{2}\right) = 2 \cdot 1 = 2$

Hence, the correct option is $(2)\ 2$.

I=∫05cosx2π-πxdx

I=∫02cos-πxdx+∫24cosπ-πxdx+∫45cos2π-πxdx

⇒I=sinπxπ02+-sinπxπ24+sinπxπ45=0

If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mi>x</mi></msubsup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfenced><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>t</mi></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi></mfrac><mi>x</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>5</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to _______.
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi></math> be a twice differentiable function on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mi>x</mi></msubsup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfenced><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>t</mi></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi></mfrac><mi>x</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>5</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to _______.
solution:
Given,

fx+∫0xx-tf'tdt=e2x+e-2xcos2x+2xa     ⋯i

Putting x=0 both side we get, f0=2     ⋯ii

On differentiating equation i w.r.t. x we get:

f'x+∫0xf'tdt+xf'x-xf'x=2e2x-e-2xcos2x-2e2x+e-2xsin2x+2a

⇒f'x+fx-f0=2e2x-e-2xcos2x-2e2x+e-2xsin2x+2a

Replace x by 0 we get:

⇒4=2a  ⇒a=12

Now putting the value of a in2a+15·a2

We get, 25·122=23=8

q: $I_n=\int_0^{\pi / 4} \tan ^n x d x$ then $\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty} n\left[I_n+I_{n-2}\right]$ equals
question: $I_n=\int_0^{\pi / 4} \tan ^n x d x$ then $\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty} n\left[I_n+I_{n-2}\right]$ equals
solution:
$\int_0^{\pi / 4} \tan ^n x\left(1+\tan ^2 x\right) d x=\int_0^{\pi / 4} \tan ^n x \sec ^2 x d x=\int_0^1 t^n d t$ where $t=\tan x$
$I_n+I_{n+2}=\frac{1}{n+1} ; \Rightarrow \operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow \infty} n\left[l_n+I_{n+2}\right]=\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow \infty} n \cdot \frac{1}{n+1}=\frac{n}{n+1}=\frac{n}{n\left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)}=1$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\mathrm{f}: \mathbf{R} \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$ be a twice differentiable function such that $f(2)=1$. If $\mathrm{F}(x)=x f(x)$ for all $x \in \mathbf{R}$, $\int_0^2 x \mathrm{~F}^{\prime}(x) \mathrm{d} x=6$ and $\int_0^2 x^2 \mathrm{~F}^{\prime \prime}(x) \mathrm{d} x=40$, then $\mathrm{F}^{\prime}(2)+\int_0^2 \mathrm{~F}(x) \mathrm{d} x$ is equal to :
question: Let $\mathrm{f}: \mathbf{R} \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$ be a twice differentiable function such that $f(2)=1$. If $\mathrm{F}(x)=x f(x)$ for all $x \in \mathbf{R}$, $\int_0^2 x \mathrm{~F}^{\prime}(x) \mathrm{d} x=6$ and $\int_0^2 x^2 \mathrm{~F}^{\prime \prime}(x) \mathrm{d} x=40$, then $\mathrm{F}^{\prime}(2)+\int_0^2 \mathrm{~F}(x) \mathrm{d} x$ is equal to :
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \int_0^2 \mathrm{xF}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=6 \\
& =\left.\mathrm{xF}(\mathrm{x})\right|_0 ^2-\int_0^2 \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=6 \\
& =2 \mathrm{~F}(2)-\int_0^2 \mathrm{xF}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=6[\therefore \mathrm{f}(2)=2 \mathrm{~F}(2)=2] \\
& \int_0^2 \mathrm{xF}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=-2 ...(1)\\
& \Rightarrow \int_0^2 \mathrm{~F}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=-2 ...(2)
\end{aligned}$

Also
$\int_0^2 x^2 F^{\prime \prime}(x) d x=\left.x^2 F^{\prime}(x)\right|_0 ^2-2 \int_0^2 x^{\prime} F^{\prime}(x) d x=40$
$\begin{aligned} & =4 F^{\prime}(2)-2 \times 6=40 \\ & F^{\prime}(2)=13 \\ & \therefore F^{\prime}(2)+\int_0^2 F(x)=13-2=11\end{aligned}$

q: The value of $\int_{-2}^3\left|1-x^2\right| d x$ is
question: The value of $\int_{-2}^3\left|1-x^2\right| d x$ is
solution: $\int_{-2}^{-1}\left(x^2-1\right) d x+\int_{-1}^1\left(1-x^2\right) d x+\int_1^3\left(x^2-1\right) d x=\frac{x^3}{3}-\left.x\right|_{-2} ^{-1}+x-\left.\frac{x^3}{3}\right|_{-1} ^1+\frac{x^3}{3}-\left.x\right|_1 ^3=\frac{28}{3}$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let fx=∫0xgtloge1-t1+tdt, where g is a continuous odd function. If ∫-π2π2fx+x2cosx1+exdx=πα2-α, then α is equal to _____.
question: Let fx=∫0xgtloge1-t1+tdt, where g is a continuous odd function. If ∫-π2π2fx+x2cosx1+exdx=πα2-α, then α is equal to _____.
solution:
Given: fx=∫0xgtlog1-t1+tdt

⇒f-x=∫0-xgtlog1-t1+tdt

⇒f-x=-∫0xg-ylog1+y1-ydy

=-∫0xg(y)ln1-y1+ydy (g is odd)

f(-x)=-f(x)⇒f is also odd.

Now,

I=∫-π2π2f(x)+x2cosx1+exdx   ...i

⇒I=∫-π2π2f(-x)+x2excosx1+exdx   ...ii

⇒2I=∫-π2π2x2cosxdx

⇒2I=2∫0π2x2cosxdx

⇒I=∫0π2x2cosxdx

⇒I=x2sinx0π2-∫0π22xsinxdx

⇒I=π24-2-xcosx+sinx0π2

⇒I=π24-2(0+1)

⇒I=π24-2

⇒I=π22-2

∴α=2

subject: Mathematics
q: The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^{x^2} \sec ^2 t d t}{x \sin x}$ is
question: The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^{x^2} \sec ^2 t d t}{x \sin x}$ is
solution:
$\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\frac{d}{d x} \int_0^{x^2} \sec ^2 t d t}{\frac{d}{d x}(x \sin x)}=\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sec ^2 x^2 \cdot 2 x}{\sin x+x \cos x}$
(by L'Hospital rule)
$\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 \sec ^2 x^2}{\left(\frac{\sin x}{x}+\cos x\right)}=\frac{2 \times 1}{1+1}=1$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $F(x)=f(x)+f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)$, where $f(x)=\int_1^x \frac{\log t}{1+t} d t$. Then $F(e)$ equals
question: Let $F(x)=f(x)+f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)$, where $f(x)=\int_1^x \frac{\log t}{1+t} d t$. Then $F(e)$ equals
solution:
$f(x)=\int_1^x \frac{\log t}{1+t} d t$
$F(e)=f(e)+f\left(\frac{1}{e}\right)$
$F(e)=\int_1^e \frac{\log t}{1+t} d t+\int_1^{1 / e} \frac{\log t}{1+t} d t$
$=\int_1^e \frac{\log t}{1+t}+\int_1^e \frac{\log t}{t(1+t)} d t$
$=\int_1^e \frac{\log t}{t} d t=\frac{1}{2}$.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mi>n</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then the integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mi>α</mi></msubsup><mfrac><msup><mi>t</mi><mn>50</mn></msup><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>. Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mi>n</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then the integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mi>α</mi></msubsup><mfrac><msup><mi>t</mi><mn>50</mn></msup><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
Let

I=∫0αt501-tdt

⇒I=-∫0α1-t50-11-tdt

⇒I=-∫0α1-t501-tdt+∫0α11-tdt

⇒I=-∫0α1+t+t2+...+t49dt-ln1-t0α

⇒I=-t+t22+t33+...+t50500α-ln1-α

⇒I=-α+α22+α33+...+α5050-ln1-α

⇒I=-β+P50α

subject: Mathematics
q: $\operatorname{IfI}(m, n)=\int_0^1 x^{m-1}(1-x)^{n-1} d x, m, n\gt0$, then $I(9,14)+I(10,13)$ is
question: $\operatorname{IfI}(m, n)=\int_0^1 x^{m-1}(1-x)^{n-1} d x, m, n\gt0$, then $I(9,14)+I(10,13)$ is
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{m})=\int_0^1 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{m}-1}(1-\mathrm{x})^{\mathrm{n}-1} d \mathrm{x} \\ & \text { Let } \mathrm{x}=\sin ^2 \theta \quad \mathrm{dx}=2 \sin \theta \cos \theta \mathrm{~d} \theta \\ & \mathrm{I}(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})=2 \int_0^{\pi / 2}(\sin \theta)^{2 \mathrm{~m}-1}(\cos \theta)^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} d \theta \\ & \mathrm{I}(9,14)+\mathrm{I}(10,13)=2 \int_0^{\pi / 2}(\sin \theta)^{17}(\cos \theta)^{27} d \theta \\ & +2 \int_0^{\pi / 2}(\sin \theta)^{19}(\cos \theta)^{25} d \theta \\ & =2 \int_0^{\pi / 2}(\sin \theta)^{17}(\cos \theta)^{25} d \theta \\ & =\mathrm{I}(9,13)\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: The value of $\int_{-\pi}^\pi \frac{2 y(1+\sin y)}{1+\cos ^2 y} d y$ is :
question: The value of $\int_{-\pi}^\pi \frac{2 y(1+\sin y)}{1+\cos ^2 y} d y$ is :
solution:
$\int_{-\pi}^\pi \frac{2 y(1+\sin y)}{1+\cos ^2 y} d y$ \(=\underset{\text{(Odd)}}{\int_{-\pi}^\pi \frac{2 y}{1+\cos ^2 y} d y}+\underset{\text{(Even)}}{\int_{-\pi}^\pi \frac{2 y \sin y}{1+\cos ^2 y} d y}\)
$=0+2.2 \int_0^\pi y\left(\frac{\sin y}{1+\cos ^2 y}\right) d y$ $\begin{aligned} & I=4 \int_0^\pi \frac{y \sin y}{1+\cos ^2 y} d y \\ & I=4 \int_0^\pi \frac{(\pi-y) \sin y}{1+\cos ^2 y} d y \\ & 2 I=4 \int_0^\pi \frac{\pi \sin y}{1+\cos ^2 y} d y \\ & I=2 \pi \int_0^\pi \frac{\sin y}{1+\cos ^2 y} d y \\ & =2 \pi\left(-\tan ^{-1}(\cos y)\right)_0^\pi \\ & =-2 \pi\left[\left(-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)-\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right] \\ & =-2 \pi\left[-\frac{2 \pi}{4}\right]=\pi^2\end{aligned}$

q: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mn>2</mn></msubsup><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> is equal to
question: The integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mn>2</mn></msubsup><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
∫02x-1-xdx=∫011-x-xdx+∫12x-1-xdx

=∫0121-2xdx+∫1212x-1dx+∫12dx

=x-x2012+x2-x121+x12=12-14-0-0+1-1-14-12+2-1

=14+14+1=32=1.5

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mn>1</mn></msubsup><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><msqrt><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>c</mi></math> are rational numbers, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>c</mi></math> is equal to :
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mn>1</mn></msubsup><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><msqrt><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>c</mi></math> are rational numbers, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>c</mi></math> is equal to :
solution:
Let, I=∫0113+x+1+xdx

⇒I=∫013+x-1+x(3+x)-(1+x)dx

⇒I=12∫013+xdx-∫01(1+x)dx

⇒I=122(3+x)323-2(1+x)32301

⇒I=122(3+1)323-2(1+1)323-2(3)323-2(1)323

⇒I=122×83-423-2×333+23

⇒I=126-423-23

⇒I=3-3-232=a+b2+c3

⇒a=3, b=-23,c=-1

⇒2 a+3 b-4 c=6-2+4=8

subject: Mathematics
q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mrow><mn>20</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></msubsup><msup><mfenced><mrow><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> is equal to:
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mrow><mn>20</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></msubsup><msup><mfenced><mrow><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> is equal to:
solution:
Let I=∫020πsinx+cosx2dx

We know that the period of sinx+cosx is π2

i.e. I=40∫0π2sinx+cosx2dx

I=40∫0π21+sin2xdx

=40x-cos2x20π2=40π2-cosπ2+cos02

I=20π+2

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following statement is correct for the function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>)</mo></math> for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mi>α</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>6</mn></mfrac><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>cos</mi><mi>α</mi></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math>
question: Which of the following statement is correct for the function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>)</mo></math> for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mi>α</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>6</mn></mfrac><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>cos</mi><mi>α</mi></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math>
solution:
gα=∫π6π3sinαxsinαx+cosαxdx …i

gα=∫π6π3cosαxsinαx+cosαxdx …ii

Adding 1+2

2gα=π3-π6=π6

⇒gα=π12

Which is constant and an even function.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let α>0. If ∫0αxx+α-xdx=16+20215 then α is equal to :
question: Let α>0. If ∫0αxx+α-xdx=16+20215 then α is equal to :
solution:
Given,

∫0αxx+α-xdx=16+20215....(1)

Rationalise the denominator in L.H.S we get,

∫0αx(α+x+x)αdx=1α∫0αxα+xdx+∫0αx3/2dx

Put α+x=t2⇒dx=2tdt

Limit when x=0→t=α, x=α→t=2α

1α∫α2αt2-α2t2dt+2α3/25

=1α2t55-2αt33α2α+2α3/25

=4α2α+10αα15

From equation 1

∫0αxx+α-xdx=16+20215

⇒4α2α+10αα15=16+20215

On comparing both sides we get,

α=2

subject: Mathematics
q: The value of the integral $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} \dfrac{\sqrt{x(x^2+x+1)}}{(\sqrt{x+1})(\sqrt{x^4+x^2+1})} \, dx$ is equal to:
question: The value of the integral $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} \dfrac{\sqrt{x(x^2+x+1)}}{(\sqrt{x+1})(\sqrt{x^4+x^2+1})} \, dx$ is equal to:
solution:
Let $I = \displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} \dfrac{\sqrt{x(x^2 + x + 1)}}{\sqrt{x + 1}\,\sqrt{x^4 + x^2 + 1}}\, dx$.

Using the identity $x^4 + x^2 + 1 = (x^2 + x + 1)(x^2 - x + 1)$:
$I = \displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} \dfrac{\sqrt{x}\,\sqrt{x^2 + x + 1}}{\sqrt{x + 1}\,\sqrt{(x^2 + x + 1)(x^2 - x + 1)}}\, dx$

Cancelling $\sqrt{x^2 + x + 1}$:
$I = \displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} \dfrac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{(x + 1)(x^2 - x + 1)}}\, dx$

Using $(x + 1)(x^2 - x + 1) = x^3 + 1$:
$I = \displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} \dfrac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x^3 + 1}}\, dx$

Substitution:
Let $t = x^{3/2}$, so $dt = \dfrac{3}{2}x^{1/2}\, dx \Rightarrow \sqrt{x}\, dx = \dfrac{2}{3}\, dt$.

Also, $t^2 = x^3$, so $x^3 + 1 = t^2 + 1$.

Changing limits:
$x = 0 \Rightarrow t = 0$
$x = 2 \Rightarrow t = 2^{3/2} = 2\sqrt{2}$

Therefore:
$I = \dfrac{2}{3}\displaystyle\int_{0}^{2\sqrt{2}} \dfrac{dt}{\sqrt{t^2 + 1}}$

Using $\displaystyle\int \dfrac{dt}{\sqrt{t^2 + a^2}} = \log_e\left|t + \sqrt{t^2 + a^2}\right| + C$:
$I = \dfrac{2}{3}\left[\log_e\left(t + \sqrt{t^2 + 1}\right)\right]_{0}^{2\sqrt{2}}$

$I = \dfrac{2}{3}\left[\log_e\left(2\sqrt{2} + \sqrt{8 + 1}\right) - \log_e(1)\right]$

$I = \dfrac{2}{3}\log_e\left(2\sqrt{2} + 3\right)$

$I = \dfrac{2}{3}\log_e\left(3 + 2\sqrt{2}\right)$

Hence, the correct option is $(3)\ \dfrac{2}{3}\log_e\left(3 + 2\sqrt{2}\right)$.

subject: Mathematics
q: If the value of the integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mn>5</mn></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo></mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo></mrow></msup></mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>5</mn><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>;</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to :
question: If the value of the integral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mn>5</mn></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo></mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo></mrow></msup></mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>5</mn><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>;</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to :
solution:
∫05x+[x]ex-[x]dx=αe-1+β

⇒∫01xexdx+∫12x+1ex-1dx+∫23x+2ex-2dx+∫34x+3ex-3dx+∫45x+4ex-4dx

Let x-1=p, x-2=q, x-3=r, x-4=w

⇒∫01xexdx+∫01p+2epdp+∫01q+4eqdq+∫01r+6erdr+∫01w+8ewdw

⇒∫01xexdx+∫01x+2exdx+∫01x+4exdx+∫01x+6exdx+∫01x+8exdx

=∫015x+20exdx=5∫01x+4exdx

=5x+4e-x-101-∫01e-x-1dx

=5-5e-1+4+-e-1+1=5-6e-1+5=-30e-1+25

α=-30,β=25

(α+β)2=25

subject: Mathematics
q: The integral $\int_{7 \pi / 4}^{7 \pi / 3} \sqrt{\tan ^2 x} d x$ is equal to :
question: The integral $\int_{7 \pi / 4}^{7 \pi / 3} \sqrt{\tan ^2 x} d x$ is equal to :
solution:
Let $\mathrm{I}=\int_{7 \pi / 4}^{7 \pi / 3} \sqrt{\tan ^2 x} d x$
$$
=\int_{7 \pi / 4}^{7 \pi / 3} \tan x d x=-\left.\log \cos x\right|_{7 \pi / 4} ^{7 \pi / 3}
$$
$=-\left[\log \cos \frac{7 \pi}{3}-\log \cos \frac{7 \pi}{4}\right]$
$=\log \cos \frac{7 \pi}{4}-\log \cos \frac{7 \pi}{3}$
$=\log \left[\frac{\cos \frac{7 \pi}{4}}{\cos \frac{7 \pi}{3}}\right]=\log \left[\frac{\cos \left(2 \pi-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}{\cos \left(2 \pi+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}\right]$
$=\log \left(\frac{\cos \frac{\pi}{4}}{\cos \frac{\pi}{3}}\right)=\log \left(\frac{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right)$
$=\log \left(\frac{2}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=\log \sqrt{2}$.

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math><mi>f</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>→</mo><mi>R</mi></math> is a differentiable function and <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></munder><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>6</mn><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mrow></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>t</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></mfrac></math> is:
question: If <math><mi>f</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>→</mo><mi>R</mi></math> is a differentiable function and <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></munder><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>6</mn><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mrow></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>t</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></mfrac></math> is:
solution:
The given limit can be written as

l=limx→2∫6fx2tdtx-2  

Applying L' Hospital’s rule i.e. if l=limx→agxhx and limx→agx→0 & limx→ahx→0, then l=limx→ag'xh'x

l=limx→2ddx∫6fx2tdt1

Now, applying Newton's Leibnitz rule i.e. ddx∫abgxdx=gb·b'-ga·a', we get

⇒l=limx→22fx·f'x-0

⇒l=2f2⋅f'2

Put the given value, to get

⇒l=2×6×f'2=12f'2.

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>α</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>1</mn><mi>α</mi></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>10</mn></msub><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><msup><mi>e</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>α</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>1</mn><mi>α</mi></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>10</mn></msub><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfrac><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><msup><mi>e</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

 fα=∫1αlog10t1+tdt

So, fe3=∫1e3lntln101+tdt     ⋯1

Again fα=∫1αlntln101+tdt

Now let t=1x⇒x=1t⇒dt=-1x2dx

So, fα=∫11a-lnnxln101+1x-1x2dx

⇒fα=1ln10∫11αlnxxx+1dx

So, fe-3=1ln10∫1e3lnttt+1dt     …2

Now adding equation 1 and 2 we get,

fe3+fe-3=1lnn10∫1c3lnt1+t1+1tdt

⇒fe3+fe-3=1ln10∫13lnttdt

Now let lnt=r⇒dtt=dr

So, fe3+fe-3=1ln10∫03rdr

⇒fe3+fe-3=1ln10r2203

⇒fe3+fe-3=1log1092

⇒fe3+fe-3=92loge10

subject: Mathematics
q: The integral $\int_0^\pi \frac{(x+3) \sin x}{1+3 \cos ^2 x} d x$ is equal to :
question: The integral $\int_0^\pi \frac{(x+3) \sin x}{1+3 \cos ^2 x} d x$ is equal to :
solution: $\begin{aligned} & I=\int_0^\pi \frac{(x+3) \sin x}{1+3 \cos ^2 x} d x \\ & I=\int_0^\pi \frac{(\pi-x+3) \sin x}{\left(1+3 \cos ^2 x\right)} d x \\ & 2 I=\int_0^\pi \frac{(\pi+6) \sin x \cdot d x}{\left(1+3 \cos ^2 x\right)}=2 \int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{(\pi+6) \sin x}{\left(1+3 \cos ^2 x\right)} \\ & I=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{(\pi+6) \sin x \cdot d x}{\left(1+3 \cos ^2 x\right)}=\frac{\pi}{3 \sqrt{3}}(\pi+6) \\ & \sqrt{3} \cos x=t \\ & \sqrt{3} \sin x=d t\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></msubsup><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> is
question: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></msubsup><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></math> is
solution:
∫-223x2-x-2dx

=3∫-22x2-x-2dx

=3∫-2-1x2-x-2dx+∫-12-x2-x-2dx

=3x33-x22-2x-2-1-x33-x22-2x-12

=37-23

=19

subject: Mathematics
q: The function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo></math> that satisfies the condition <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mrow><mi>π</mi><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msubsup><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>cos</mi><mi>y</mi><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>)</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>,</mo></math> is :
question: The function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo></math> that satisfies the condition <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mrow><mi>π</mi><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msubsup><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>cos</mi><mi>y</mi><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>)</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>,</mo></math> is :
solution:
Given the function fx=x+∫0π2sinx·cosyfydy

fx=x+ksinx   ...i

fy=y+ksiny   ...ii

 k=∫0π2cosyfydy

From equations i & ii

fx=x+∫0π2sinxcosyy+ksinydy

fx=x+sinx∫0π2ycosydy+k2sinx∫0π2sin2ydy

fx=x+sinxy∫0π2cosydy-∫0π2ddyy∫0π2cosydydy+k2sinx∫0π2sin2ydy

fx=x+sinxπ2-1+k2sinx×1

fx=x+(π-2)sinx2+ksinx2   ...iii

from (i) and (iii)

k=π2-1+k2

k=π-2

Hence,  f(x)=x+(π-2)sinx
